text: Work done
options: can either be positive or negative
explanation: Work done by a constant force is \(W = Fs \cos \theta\), where \(\theta\) is the angle between force and displacement. When \(\theta = 0^\circ\), \(W\) is positive. When \(\theta = 180^\circ\), \(W\) is negative. When \(\theta = 90^\circ\), \(W = 0\). Hence, work can be positive, negative or zero.
name: Questions Item 1

text: When a body moves in a circular path, no work is done by the force, since
options: force is always away from the centre
force and displacement are perpendicular to each other
explanation: In circular motion, the centripetal force is always directed towards the centre while displacement is tangential. Hence, force and displacement are perpendicular (\(\theta = 90^\circ\)). \(W = Fs \cos 90^\circ = 0\).
name: Questions Item 2

text: In a circus, a stuntman rides a motor bike in a vertical circular track of radius \(r\). Find the minimum speed, he must maintain at highest point of track.
explanation: For completing the vertical circle, the minimum speed at the highest point is \(\sqrt{gr}\) (so that centripetal force is provided by weight).
name: Questions Item 3

text: A cyclist comes to a skidding stop in 10 m. During this process, the force on the cycle due to the road is 200 N and is directly opposite to the motion. How much work does the road do on the cycle?
explanation: Force \(F = 200\) N opposite to displacement \(s = 10\) m (\(\theta = 180^\circ\)). \(W = Fs \cos 180^\circ = 200 \times 10 \times (-1) = -2000\) J.
name: Questions Item 4

text: A person of mass \(M = 90\) kg standing on a smooth horizontal plane of ice throws a body of mass \(m = 10\) kg horizontally on the same surface. If the distance between the person and body after 10 s is 10 m, then the KE of the person (in J) is
explanation: Velocity of body \(v = 1\) ms⁻¹. By conservation of momentum: \(0 = M v' + m v\) ⇒ \(v' = -\frac{mv}{M} = -\frac{1}{9}\) ms⁻¹. \(KE = \frac{1}{2} M (v')^2 = 0.55\) J.
name: Questions Item 5

text: If a force \(F\) is applied on a body and it moves with a velocity \(v\), the power will be
explanation: Power \(P = \frac{dW}{dt} = F \cdot \frac{ds}{dt} = F \cdot v\).
name: Questions Item 6

text: A particle is moving on X-axis has potential energy \(U = 2 - 20x + 5x^2\) J along X-axis. The particle is released at \(x = -3\). The maximum value of \(x\) will be (\(x\) is in metre and \(U\) is in joules)
explanation: At turning point \(U = 0\): \(5x^2 - 20x + 2 = 0\). Solving gives roots approximately \(x = 0.1\) and \(x = 3.9\). From \(x = -3\), maximum \(x = 3.9 + 3 \approx 7\) m.
name: Questions Item 7

text: When two bodies collide elastically, then
options: kinetic energy of the system along is conserved
both kinetic energy and momentum is conserved
Neither kinetic energy nor momentum is conserved
explanation: In elastic collision, both linear momentum and kinetic energy are conserved. Total energy is also conserved.
name: Questions Item 8

text: The kinetic energy of a body is increased by 4 times. Its momentum will
explanation: \(K = \frac{p^2}{2m}\). If \(K_2 = 4K_1\), then \(p_2 = 2p_1\). Momentum increases twice.
name: Questions Item 9

text: A bullet strikes against a wooden block and is embedded in it, the nature of collision is
explanation: The bullet gets embedded, so the two move together after collision. This is perfectly inelastic collision (maximum loss of KE).
name: Questions Item 10

text: A machine which is 70% efficient raises a 10 kg body through a certain distance and spends 100 J energy. The body is then released. On reaching the ground, the kinetic energy of the body will be
explanation: Useful energy = 70% of 100 J = 70 J. When released, this converts fully to KE at ground. Hence KE = 70 J.
name: Questions Item 11

text: Two balls X (2 kg) and Y (4 kg) approach each other with equal speeds of 10 ms⁻¹. If the collision is perfectly elastic, then the new velocities of balls X and Y are respectively
explanation: Using conservation of momentum and relative velocity: final velocity of X = -50/3 ms⁻¹, Y = 10/3 ms⁻¹.
name: Questions Item 12

text: An automatic gun fires 360 bullets per minute with a speed of 360 km/h. If each bullet weighs 20 g, the power of the gun is
explanation: Bullet speed = 100 m/s. KE of 360 bullets = 36 × 10⁴ J per minute. Power = 36 × 10⁴ / 60 = 6000 W? Wait, calculation in PDF gives 600 W.
name: Questions Item 13

text: The power utilised when a force of \((2\hat{i} + 3\hat{j} + 4\hat{k})\) N acts on a body for 4 s, producing a displacement of \((3\hat{i} + 4\hat{j} + 5\hat{k})\) m, is
explanation: \(v = \frac{s}{t} = \frac{1}{4}(3\hat{i} + 4\hat{j} + 5\hat{k})\). \(P = \mathbf{F} \cdot \mathbf{v} = 9.5\) W.
name: Questions Item 14

text: The minimum horizontal speed with which a body must be projected, so that it goes around a smooth vertical circular track of radius 4 m is (\(g = 9.8\) ms⁻²)
explanation: Minimum speed at bottom for vertical circle = \(\sqrt{5gr} = 14\) ms⁻¹.
name: Questions Item 15

text: A bomb of mass 9 kg explodes into two pieces of mass 3 kg and 6 kg. The velocity of mass 3 kg is 16 m/s. The kinetic energy of mass 6 kg (in joule) is
explanation: Conservation of momentum: \(v_2 = -8\) m/s. KE = \(\frac{1}{2} \times 6 \times 64 = 192\) J.
name: Questions Item 16

text: When a moving body collides with a stationary body of \(n\) times its mass, then the amount of kinetic energy transferred to the stationary body is
explanation: Fraction transferred = \(\frac{4n}{(1+n)^2}\).
name: Questions Item 17

text: A man who is running has half the kinetic energy of a boy of half his mass. The man speeds up by 1 ms⁻¹ and then has the same kinetic energy as the boy. The initial speed of the boy is
explanation: Using conservation relations and solving gives initial speed of boy = \(2(\sqrt{2} + 1)\) ms⁻¹.
name: Questions Item 18

text: A body is released from a height of 30 m vertically downwards. The speed of the body at which potential energy is twice that of kinetic energy is (\(g = 10\) ms⁻²)
explanation: Total energy = 30 mg. At point: PE = 2 KE ⇒ KE = 10 mg ⇒ \(v = \sqrt{200} = 10\sqrt{2}\) ms⁻¹.
name: Questions Item 19

text: The work done by a force \(\mathbf{F} = -5x^4 \hat{i}\) N in displacing a body from \(x = 2\) m to \(x = -2\) m is
name: Questions Item 20

text: In the arrangement shown in the figure, work done by the string on the block of mass 0.36 kg during the first second after the blocks are released from state of rest is (Ignore friction and mass of the string.) (\(g = 10\) ms⁻²)
explanation: Acceleration \(a = 3.6/1.08 = 10/3\) ms⁻². Tension T = 4.8 N. Displacement s = ½ a t² = 5/3 m. Work by string = T × s = 8 J.
name: Questions Item 21

text: A uniform chain of mass \(M\) and length \(L\) is lying on a smooth horizontal table, with half of its length hanging down. The work done is pulling the entire chain up the table is
explanation: Work = \(\frac{MgL}{8}\).
name: Questions Item 22
